name: DatePicker

unions.name: Msg
unions.comment: A message type for the date picker.

aliases.name: Model
aliases.comment:
A model to track the state of the date picker.

An instance of this should appear somewhere in your applications model.
aliases.type: { focused : Date, choice : Choice, selected : Maybe Date, selectedEnd : Maybe Date, config : Config }

values.name: getNow
values.comment:
Set the focus to the current date.

This can be used to initialize the date picker.

    init : Config -> ( Model, Cmd Msg )
    init config =
        ( (DatePicker.initWithConfig config), DatePicker.getNow Msg )

values.name: init
values.comment: An initial state with nothing selected and a default configuration.

values.name: initWithConfig
values.comment:
Initialize the data picker with nothing selected and a custom configuration.

Custom configurations can be used to override the styling, placeholder strings,
and whether the picker selects a single date or a range.

name: DatePicker.Config

aliases.name: Config
aliases.comment: The configuration for a date picker.
aliases.type: { getClasses : View -> List ( String, Bool ), useDefaultStyles : Bool, defaultDate : Date, useRange : Bool, placeholderFrom : String, placeholderTo : String }

values.name: setDefaultDate
values.comment: Update a configuration to use the specified default date.

values.name: setGetClasses
values.comment: Update a configuration to use the specified function to control the styling.

values.name: setUseDefaultStyles
values.comment: Update whether a configuration should use the default styles.

values.name: setUseRange
values.comment: Update a configuration to select either a range or a single date.

name: DatePicker.Style

unions.name: View
unions.comment: All of the view fragments that can be styled.

values.name: getDefaultStyle
values.comment: A default style for the datepicker.
